name: West Texas National Bank
description_short: West Texas National Bank is a regional bank serving the Permian Basin since 1904, offering personal, commercial, energy, and agricultural banking services with multiple branch locations across West Texas.
description_long:
West Texas National Bank has been operating since 1904, establishing itself as a deeply rooted financial institution in West Texas with headquarters in Midland. The bank has built its reputation on understanding the region's unique economic drivers, particularly the energy sector and agricultural industries that define the Permian Basin economy.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of banking services including personal banking (checking, savings, mortgages), commercial banking, small business banking, energy sector lending, agribusiness lending, commercial real estate financing, and treasury cash management solutions. Customers can access accounts through online banking available 24/7, multiple physical branch locations, ATMs with deposit capabilities, and customer service via phone.

What distinguishes West Texas National Bank is its specialized expertise in energy banking and its position as Midland-headquartered and Midland-owned. The bank markets itself specifically to the Permian Basin energy industry with a dedicated Energy Banking Team and emphasizes its deep regional relationships and understanding of local business needs. Their agribusiness lending division offers customized solutions for farmers and ranchers, reflecting regional expertise beyond typical banking.

As a regional bank, WTNB provides traditional banking services with local decision-making and personalized service, though as a smaller institution it may have fewer digital features than larger national banks. Recent branch consolidations (Summit location eliminating drive-through and ATM services as of March 30, 2026) indicate the bank is adjusting its footprint. For customers seeking relationship-based banking with energy or agricultural focus in West Texas, this institution offers relevant expertise. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: ATM access with cash and check deposit capability
pros: 120+ year history serving West Texas, providing institutional stability and deep regional knowledge
Specialized Energy Banking Team serving the Permian Basin with dedicated expertise
Customized agribusiness lending for farmers, ranchers, and agricultural operations
Multiple branch locations across West Texas (Alpine, Crane, Denver City, Kermit, Lubbock, Midland, Odessa, Pecos, Plainview)
ATMs at most locations accept both cash and check deposits for convenience
Treasury Cash Management solutions for business customers
Locally owned and headquartered in Midland, supporting regional decision-making
cons: Summit location eliminating drive-through, night deposit, and ATM services effective March 30, 2026, reducing convenience for existing customers
Limited branch hours compared to larger banks (most locations close at 3-4 p.m. weekdays, no weekend hours mentioned)
No Saturday or Sunday banking hours listed for any location
Odessa location has no drive-through service available
Regional focus limits accessibility for customers outside West Texas market
best_for: Energy sector businesses and professionals working in the Permian Basin
Farmers, ranchers, and agribusiness operators needing specialized agricultural lending
West Texas residents and businesses seeking relationship-based banking with local expertise
Commercial real estate investors and developers in the West Texas region
diagnosis: West Texas National Bank is best for energy sector professionals, agricultural operators, and regional businesses in the Permian Basin who value relationship banking and specialized industry expertise. The main caveat is that recent branch consolidations and limited operating hours reflect a regional bank adjusting its footprint, and service availability is concentrated in West Texas—making it less suitable for customers requiring broad geographic reach or extended banking hours.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
